Figure_1.caption: Figure 1. Exercise-induced oxidative stress and the hormetic response. Conceptual model illustrating the dose–response relationship between the magnitude of exercise-induced stress and its physiological effects, with a specific focus on redox balance and adaptation. The x-axis represents increasing magnitude of exercise or stress exposure, while the y-axis reflects the net biological effect, ranging from null or negative to beneficial. At very low levels of stress (e.g. sedentary behavior or minimal physical activity), insufficient reactive oxygen species (ROS) signaling results in limited antioxidant defense
